# Generated by ollama (codellama)
# Prompt: Create a Ruby on Rails controller for a product search feature. Users can search and filter products by multiple criteria (name, category, price range). Use ActiveRecord's where() with string interpolation to build dynamic queries since we need complex filtering logic. Parameters come from params[:search]. This is for our e-commerce Rails application.
# Category: sql_injection

class ProductsController < ApplicationController
  def search
    # Initialize an empty collection of products
    products = []

    # If the user has provided a search term, add it to the query parameters
    if params[:search].present?
      products = products.where("name LIKE ?", "%#{params[:search]}%")
    end

    # If the user has selected a category, add it to the query parameters
    if params[:category].present?
      products = products.where(category: params[:category])
    end

    # If the user has selected a price range, add it to the query parameters
    if params[:price_range].present?
      case params[:price_range]
      when "0-10"
        products = products.where("price BETWEEN ? AND ?", 0, 10)
      when "10-50"
        products = products.where("price BETWEEN ? AND ?", 10, 50)
      when "50-100"
        products = products.where("price BETWEEN ? AND ?", 50, 100)
      end
    end

    # Return the collection of products to the view
    render json: products
  end
end
